title: Deferred Annuity
event: Hollis Co. will receive four annual payments of $5,000, with the first payment at the end of year 3 (a two-year deferral), at 10% (PV-OA factor, 10%, 4 = 3.16987; PV single-sum factor, 10%, 2 = 0.82645). Compute the present value today.

computationPaths.narration: Two steps: value the annuity as of the period BEFORE its first payment, then discount that lump back through the deferral.

memorize.body: The ordinary-annuity factor lands you ONE PERIOD BEFORE the first payment — first payment end of year 3 means the annuity value sits at end of year 2.
Forgetting the second discount is the classic slip — the 15,849 is a year-2 value, not a today value.

questions.prompt: Four payments of $5,000 starting end of year 3, 10%. PV today = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the value at the END OF YEAR 2. Discount it two more years: × 0.82645 = 13,099.
Undiscounted total. Every payment must be discounted — through the deferral too.
